input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's a complete `AppShell.xaml` setup:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two sub-tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**

```csharp
namespace MyMauiApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register routes not in the visual hierarchy
        Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Key points:**

- **`FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"`** enables the hamburger menu.
- The `<Tab>` wrapper inside the Products `FlyoutItem` gives you the bottom tab bar with Active/Archived.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until the user navigates there.
- `ProductDetailsPage` is registered as a *global route* (not in the visual tree), so you navigate to it from either product list with:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails", new Dictionary<string, object>
{
    ["Product"] = selectedProduct
});
```

and receive the parameter in `ProductDetailsPage` via `[QueryProperty]` or `IQueryAttributable`.
